module_title: Stressors Affecting Mood (Depression and Bipolar Disorder)

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Mood disorders such as depression and bipolar illness impact energy, emotion, and functioning. This overview highlights how nurses support patients experiencing mood shifts.

sections.title: Major Depressive Disorder
sections.content: Persistent sadness, loss of interest, sleep disturbance, and hopelessness characterize MDD. Screening and early treatment improve outcomes.

sections.title: Bipolar Disorder
sections.content: Bipolar illness features cycles of mania and depression. Mood stabilizers and therapy help control episodes and promote stability.

sections.title: Suicide Risk Assessment
sections.content: Assess intent, plan, and protective factors. Take all suicidal statements seriously and ensure safety through supervision and support.

sections.title: Psychopharmacology
sections.content: Antidepressants, mood stabilizers like lithium, and antipsychotics are common medications. Monitor side effects and teach adherence.

sections.title: Electroconvulsive Therapy
sections.content: ECT may relieve severe or treatment-resistant depression. Nurses prepare the patient, obtain consent, and monitor recovery.

sections.title: Managing Manic Behaviors
sections.content: During mania, set limits calmly, reduce environmental stimuli, and promote nutrition and rest.

sections.title: Ongoing Care and Education
sections.content: Teaching about symptom monitoring, medication management, and community support groups fosters long-term wellness.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: Understanding mood disorders equips nurses to offer compassionate care and suicide prevention strategies.
